title: Ensure that Service Account Tokens are only mounted  where necessary

input_row.title: Ensure that Service Account Tokens are only mounted  where necessary
input_row.description: Service accounts tokens should not be mounted in pods except where the workload running in the pod explicitly needs to communicate with the API server
input_row.rationale: Mounting service account tokens inside pods can provide an avenue for privilege escalation attacks where an attacker is able to compromise a single pod in the cluster. Avoiding mounting these tokens removes this attack avenue.
input_row.audit: Review pod and service account objects in the cluster and ensure that the option below is set, unless the resource explicitly requires this access. automountServiceAccountToken: false
input_row.remediation: Modify the definition of pods and service accounts which do not need to mount service account tokens to disable it.
input_row.impact: Pods mounted without service account tokens will not be able to communicate with the API server, except where the resource is available to unauthenticated principals.
input_row.default_value: By default, all pods get a service account token mounted in them.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The rationale emphasizes avoiding privilege escalation via compromised pod tokens. The `audit` field specifies checking the pod and service account objects for the setting `automountServiceAccountToken: false`, which directly links to accessible properties in the GCP APIs.
- The GCP Kubernetes API allows for querying pod configurations to verify whether service account tokens are being mounted.
- GCP SDK (`gcloud`) commands can be used to retrieve and inspect the configurations of pods within a GKE cluster.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_gke_cluster_service_account_token_auto_mount_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Kubernetes API to list all pods in the GKE cluster: `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o json`.
- For each pod, check the configuration for the field `automountServiceAccountToken`. The check should identify if this is set to `false` or not present.
- If `automountServiceAccountToken` is set to `true` or not specified (which defaults to `true`), it fails the compliance check.
- The GCP API endpoint `GET /api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/pods/{name}` could be utilized to inspect individual pod resource configurations.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - **Pass:** If `automountServiceAccountToken` is set to `false`.
  - **Fail:** If `automountServiceAccountToken` is set to `true` or not specified (default behavior).
- This automated check helps ensure that service account tokens are only mounted when necessary, thus following the CIS benchmark recommendation.
